title: Calcitrapa lanuginosa
taxonomy: Calcitrapa lanuginosa is a member of the family Brassicaceae. This family includes a wide variety of plants such as broccoli, cauliflower, and mustard. Calcitrapa lanuginosa is a type of flowering plant and is commonly known as woolly caltrop. The classification of Calcitrapa lanuginosa is further divided into the genera and species levels. It is also known to belong to the order Brassicales.
uses: Calcitrapa lanuginosa has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and roots are said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in soothing skin irritations and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of Calcitrapa lanuginosa.

intro: Calcitrapa lanuginosa, also known as Nasturtium or Indian Cress, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Calcitrapa lanuginosa include isothiocyanates, which are responsible for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, often combined with other herbs like peppermint and eucalyptus to create topical remedies for colds and flu.
